content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — both purpose strings are generic/placeholder: `NSCameraUsageDescription` is "This app needs camera access." and `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` is literally "TODO" (App/Info.plist:8,10).
Pierre: Apple rejects vague or placeholder purpose strings because users must understand why data is collected before granting access. The camera string doesn't mention the visible document-scanning feature in ScannerView.swift, and the location string is an unfinished "TODO" with no corresponding location code anywhere in the project. Rewrite the camera string to reference document scanning (e.g. "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and capture your documents"), and either add a real, feature-tied location string or remove the key entirely if no location feature exists.
